source: Curated from quantumalgorithmzoo.org + Troyer framework analysis (expanded April 2026)

algorithms.name: Shor's Algorithm
algorithms.notes: The canonical example. Breaks RSA. Needs ~4000 logical qubits for RSA-2048.

algorithms.name: Quantum Phase Estimation (QPE)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for naturally quantum Hamiltonians. Core of quantum chemistry advantage.

algorithms.name: Hamiltonian Simulation (Product Formulas)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for simulating quantum systems. Feynman's original motivation.

algorithms.name: Quantum Walk (Transport)
algorithms.classical_best: Lindblad master equation (classical simulation of open quantum systems)
algorithms.notes: Exponential speedup for naturally quantum transport. Avoids I/O problem.

algorithms.name: Lattice Gauge Theory (Real-Time)
algorithms.classical_best: Lattice QCD (Euclidean only, sign problem for real-time)
algorithms.notes: Classical lattice QCD cannot do real-time dynamics (sign problem). Strongest long-term utility case.

algorithms.name: Grover's Search
algorithms.notes: Provably optimal quadratic speedup but QRAM loading O(N) erases it. Oracle cost for real problems (AES, database) dominates.

algorithms.name: Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE)
algorithms.notes: Quadratic speedup proven. But QEC overhead requires ε < 10⁻⁴ for net advantage. Distribution loading is O(N).

algorithms.name: HHL Algorithm
algorithms.notes: Exponential core speedup but O(N) state prep and O(N) readout on both ends. Only useful if you need the quantum state, not the classical solution vector.

algorithms.name: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE)
algorithms.notes: No proven speedup. Barren plateaus at scale. Classical tensor networks often competitive. Use QPE instead for fault-tolerant era.

algorithms.name: QAOA (Quantum Approximate Optimization)
algorithms.notes: No proven advantage beyond quadratic. Constant-depth QAOA is classically simulable. GW achieves 0.878-approx for MaxCut in poly time.

algorithms.name: Quantum Machine Learning (Kernel Methods)
algorithms.notes: Exponential kernel speedup exists but O(N) data loading negates it. Proven advantages only for specific contrived problems.

algorithms.name: Quantum Error Correction (Surface Code)
algorithms.quantum_complexity: O(d²) physical qubits per logical qubit
algorithms.notes: Not an algorithm — enabling technology. Threshold theorem guarantees fault-tolerant computation if physical error rate < threshold.

algorithms.name: Discrete Logarithm
algorithms.notes: Breaks Diffie-Hellman and ECDSA. Same core technique as Shor (hidden subgroup over Z).

algorithms.name: Abelian Hidden Subgroup
algorithms.notes: Generalizes Shor, Simon, Deutsch-Jozsa. Core primitive for period finding. Uses QFT over finite Abelian groups.

algorithms.name: Quantum Cryptanalysis
algorithms.notes: Breaks RSA, DSA, ECDSA, Diffie-Hellman. Subexponential attack on isogeny-based crypto. Polynomial speedups against symmetric ciphers via Grover.

algorithms.name: Pell's Equation
algorithms.notes: Hallgren 2002. Solves x^2 - dy^2 = 1. Breaks Buchman-Williams cryptosystem. Reduces to Abelian hidden subgroup.

algorithms.name: Bernstein-Vazirani
algorithms.notes: Finds hidden bit string with single query. Recursive version gives exponential separation. Foundational for quantum learning theory.

algorithms.name: Deutsch-Jozsa
algorithms.notes: First quantum algorithm showing speedup. Exponential deterministic advantage but no advantage over randomized classical. Historical importance.

algorithms.name: Simon's Algorithm
algorithms.notes: Finds hidden period of 2-to-1 function. Inspired Shor's algorithm. First provable exponential quantum speedup for promise problem.

algorithms.name: Collision Finding
algorithms.notes: Brassard-Hoyer-Tapp. Finds collisions in 2-to-1 functions. Only cubic root speedup. Used in quantum attacks on hash functions.

algorithms.name: Element Distinctness
algorithms.notes: Ambainis quantum walk algorithm. Optimal. Generalizes to k-distinctness. Foundation for many quantum walk-based algorithms.

algorithms.name: Welded Tree / Graph Traversal
algorithms.notes: Childs et al. Continuous-time quantum walk traverses welded binary trees exponentially faster. Provable exponential separation in oracle model.

algorithms.name: Formula Evaluation (NAND Trees)
algorithms.notes: Farhi-Goldstone-Gutmann quantum walk on NAND trees. Generalized by Reichardt span programs. Grover search is special case.

algorithms.name: Hidden Shift
algorithms.notes: van Dam-Hallgren-Ip. Exponential speedup for shifted Legendre symbols and multiplicative characters.

algorithms.name: Quantum Singular Value Transformation (QSVT)
algorithms.notes: Gilyen et al. Unifying framework subsuming Hamiltonian simulation, QPE, HHL, amplitude amplification.

algorithms.name: Preparing Eigenstates and Thermal States
algorithms.notes: Quantum Gibbs sampling, adiabatic state preparation, imaginary time evolution. Essential for quantum chemistry.

algorithms.name: Open Quantum System Simulation
algorithms.notes: Simulates Lindblad master equations, dissipative dynamics. Relevant for quantum optics, quantum biology, decoherence.

algorithms.name: Knot and 3-Manifold Invariants
algorithms.notes: Jones polynomial at roots of unity is BQP-complete (Freedman-Kitaev-Wang). Deep connection between QC and topology.

algorithms.name: Partition Functions
algorithms.notes: Evaluating Potts model, Ising model partition functions. Tutte polynomial special cases.

algorithms.name: Quantum Field Theory Simulation
algorithms.classical_best: Sign problem for real-time; exponential for fermions
algorithms.notes: Jordan-Lee-Preskill framework. Simulates scattering in scalar and fermionic QFTs.

algorithms.name: Simulated Annealing (Quantum Speedup)
algorithms.notes: Somma-Boixo. Quadratic speedup over classical simulated annealing via quantum walk on Markov chains.

algorithms.name: Probabilistic Sampling (Quantum Supremacy)
algorithms.notes: BosonSampling, random circuit sampling. Basis of quantum supremacy demonstrations.

algorithms.name: Adiabatic Quantum Computation
algorithms.quantum_complexity: O(1/gamma^2) where gamma is spectral gap
algorithms.notes: Equivalent to circuit model with non-stoquastic Hamiltonians. Gap analysis notoriously hard.

algorithms.name: Gradient Estimation
algorithms.notes: Jordan 2005. Single query estimates gradient. O(d) speedup for quadratic minimization.

algorithms.name: Semidefinite Programming (Quantum)
algorithms.notes: Brandao-Svore. Quadratic speedup when rank r is small. Includes LP as special case.

algorithms.name: Convex Optimization
algorithms.notes: General quadratic speedup in dimension for convex optimization and volume estimation.

algorithms.name: Decoded Quantum Interferometry (DQI)
algorithms.notes: Jordan et al. (Nature 2025). Reduces optimization to decoding via QFT. Apparent exponential speedup for polynomial regression over finite fields.

algorithms.name: Linear Differential Equations (Quantum)
algorithms.notes: Berry 2014. Exponential speedup in N but O(N) state prep + readout.

algorithms.name: Nonlinear Differential Equations (Quantum)
algorithms.notes: Carleman linearization approach. Limited to weakly nonlinear regime. Readout is O(N).

algorithms.name: Quantum Monte Carlo Speedup
algorithms.notes: Montanaro 2015. Quadratic speedup for MC integration. Applied to finance and fluid dynamics. I/O + QEC overhead limits advantage.

algorithms.name: Quantum Topological Data Analysis
algorithms.notes: Lloyd-Garnerone-Zanardi. Computes Betti numbers. Data loading bottleneck. Partially dequantizable.

algorithms.name: Quantum Support Vector Machine
algorithms.notes: Rebentrost-Mohseni-Lloyd. Exponential kernel speedup IF data in QRAM. O(N) data loading negates advantage. Partially dequantized.

algorithms.name: Quantum Recommendation Systems
algorithms.notes: Kerenidis-Prakash, dequantized by Tang 2018. Landmark result showing classical sampling matches quantum.

algorithms.name: Quantum Dynamic Programming
algorithms.notes: Ambainis et al. Combines Grover + DP. Speeds up TSP, vertex ordering, minimum set cover.

algorithms.name: Quantum Backtracking
algorithms.notes: Montanaro 2015. General quantum speedup for backtracking via quantum walks. Applies to CSPs, SAT, graph coloring.

algorithms.name: Lattice Problems (Quantum)
algorithms.classical_best: No known poly-time for these regimes
algorithms.notes: Chen-Liu-Zhandry 2022. Does NOT break main post-quantum cryptosystems at standard parameters.

algorithms.name: Coupled Classical Oscillators Simulation
algorithms.notes: Babbush et al. (PRX 2024). Exponential speedup for coupled harmonic oscillators on expander graphs. System is classical.
